description: Region types drawn from specific real-world cultures and traditions.
regions: A sun-drenched domain of domed palaces and spice-laden markets, ruled by a sultan's absolute word.
A theocratic expanse united under a caliph's spiritual and temporal authority, spanning deserts and cities alike.
Windswept steppe territory claimed by a mounted warlord, its borders shifting with the herds.
A jewel of arid civilization governed by an emir, thriving on oasis trade and desert cunning.
A vast, snow-buried realm ruled by an iron-willed tsar from a fortress of onion domes.
A maritime empire whose power flows from warships and harbors rather than fields and castles.
A provincial territory administered by a governor answering to a distant imperial throne.
A regional seat of military and civil power, its pasha dispensing justice from a fortified citadel.
A chieftain's hard-won territory of hill country and raiding trails, loyal to strength alone.
